title: AGENTS.md — XGeoRTR

title: AGENTS.md — XGeoRTR
text: Canonical public backend object xgeo_state. Public XGeoRTR naming stays xgeo_* . XGeoRTR exports backend state/contracts export renderer adapters. ggWebGL owns adapter assembly rendering xgeo_state. scene, camera, viewport, layers, theme, render_backend, shader/widget/snapshot/export surfaces.

title: AGENTS.md — XGeoRTR
text: Define core object contracts. Implement first operator layer. Document downstream consumption patterns.

title: Interface Freeze - XGeoRTR and ggWebGL
text: One-way boundary: XGeoRTR produces backend state analytics. ggWebGL consumes backend state renderer adapters owns rendering. package may semantic geometry state rendering behavior.

title: Interface Freeze - XGeoRTR and ggWebGL
text: Canonical public object: xgeo_state geometry$points canonical point universe required columns: point_id, x, y optional coordinate: z explanations, point_meta, feature_meta, predictions, uncertainty embeddings, diagnostics, baseline, method, structure embedding coordinate tables keyed point_id active embedding selection backend state, renderer viewport state point_ids, feature_ids consistent geometry/feature tables point_ids, features semantic selection ; hover, brushing UI, event handling belong frontends named bundles embedding, color_by, default_level, levels active bundle pointer backend multiscale summaries ; WebGL texture, color, shader, camera policy metadata Frozen public API: xgeo_state, as_xgeo_state, validate_xgeo_state compute_xgeo_embedding, compute_xgeo_diagnostics, build_xgeo_lod set_active_embedding, set_xgeo_selection, set_xgeo_lod read_xgeo_state, write_xgeo_state xgeo_geometry, xgeo_attributes, xgeo_indices, xgeo_selection, xgeo_metadata xgeo_explanation_table, xgeo_point_values, xgeo_regular_grid Serialization freeze: JSON root key state schema version controlled XGeoRTR write_xgeo_state() read_xgeo_state() exchange backend JSON renderer fields never serialized Forbidden public state semantics: scene, camera, viewport, layers, theme, render_backend shader/widget/snapshot/export renderer surfaces

title: Interface Freeze - XGeoRTR and ggWebGL
text: Canonical entrypoint ggWebGL: as_ggwebgl_spec.xgeo_state(state, ...) Adapter input controls: embedding choice (active default) primitive choice (points, density, surface) optional LOD selector (bundle/level) renderer style options Adapter output target: ggWebGL render contract (render$mode, render$panels, primitive payloads, counts, messages) renderer-ready points, lines, raster layers coordinates, color/alpha, size/width, grouping, panel assignment already resolved explanation semantics required ggWebGL

title: Interface Freeze - XGeoRTR and ggWebGL
text: XGeoRTR owns: state model, coercion, validation embeddings, diagnostics, LOD computation selected long explanation tables, point aggregate tables, regular-grid validation backend serialization ggWebGL owns: renderer scene assembly/spec realization primitive rasterization shaders widget lifecycle, snapshots, exports, interaction, camera/viewport

title: Interface Freeze - XGeoRTR and ggWebGL
text: XGeoRTR backend functions use: xgeo_* as_xgeo_* compute_xgeo_* set_xgeo_* build_xgeo_* read_xgeo_state() write_xgeo_state() Reserved outside XGeoRTR core: scene, render, shader, camera, viewport, panel, layer, widget, snapshot Use XGeoRTR-owned nouns instead: state, embedding, diagnostics, lod, selection, view_data

title: Interface Freeze - XGeoRTR and ggWebGL
text: XGeoRTR installs, loads, passes core tests without ggWebGL installed. XGeoRTR public objects backend-neutral explainable-geometry/state objects. XGeoRTR public objects serialized payloads contain renderer-owned scene graphs, layers, viewport/camera state, canvas/UI state, themes, shaders, backend-selection metadata. ggWebGL renders normalized primitive-scene adapter payloads without understanding explanation semantics. XGeoRTR depend ggWebGL. Downstream packages can consume XGeoRTR state optionally use ggWebGL views explicit adapter contracts. Research submission materials can state: XGeoRTR explainable-geometry backend; ggWebGL reusable browser-native renderer.

title: Getting Started with XGeoRTR
code: library(XGeoRTR)  demo_path <- system.file("extdata", "spatial_demo.csv", package = "XGeoRTR") demo_tbl <- utils::read.csv(demo_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  state <- as_xgeo_state(   demo_tbl,   x_col = "x",   y_col = "y",   z_col = "z",   value_col = "value",   feature_col = "feature",   method = "spatial-field-demo",   meta = list(source = "synthetic-demo", sample_id = "grid-01") )  state #> <xgeo_state> #>   structure:    spatial #>   method:       spatial-field-demo #>   points:       16 #>   features:     16 #>   embeddings:   1 (active: spatial) #>   diagnostics:  0 #>   lod bundles:  0 summary(state) #> <summary.xgeo_state> #>   structure:      spatial #>   method:         spatial-field-demo #>   points:         16 #>   features:       16 #>   explanations:   16 #>   embeddings:     1 (active: spatial) #>   diagnostics:    0 (active: none) #>   lod bundles:    0 (active: none) #>   selected points:0 #>   selected feats: 0

title: Getting Started with XGeoRTR
code: state <- compute_xgeo_embedding(state, method = "pca", source = "points", dims = 2) state <- set_active_embedding(state, "pca_points") state <- compute_xgeo_diagnostics(state, embedding = "pca_points", source = "points", k = 3) state <- build_xgeo_lod(state, embedding = "pca_points", levels = c(8L, 16L), auto_threshold = 10L) state <- set_xgeo_selection(state, point_ids = state$indices$point_ids[[1]])  summary(state) #> <summary.xgeo_state> #>   structure:      spatial #>   method:         spatial-field-demo #>   points:         16 #>   features:       16 #>   explanations:   16 #>   embeddings:     2 (active: pca_points) #>   diagnostics:    1 (active: diagnostics_pca_points_points) #>   lod bundles:    1 (active: density_grid_pca_points) #>   selected points:1 #>   selected feats: 0

title: Getting Started with XGeoRTR
code: names(xgeo_geometry(state)) #> [1] "points" names(xgeo_attributes(state)) #>  [1] "explanations" "point_meta"   "feature_meta" "predictions"  "uncertainty"  #>  [6] "embeddings"   "diagnostics"  "baseline"     "method"       "structure" names(xgeo_indices(state)) #> [1] "point_ids"   "feature_ids" xgeo_selection(state) #> $point_ids #> [1] "point_1" #>  #> $features #> character(0) names(xgeo_metadata(state)) #> [1] "source"    "sample_id"

code: long_tbl <- xgeo_explanation_table(state) point_tbl <- xgeo_point_values(state) grid <- xgeo_regular_grid(point_tbl)  utils::head(long_tbl) #>   point_id  feature value x y z    label #> 1  point_1 cell_1_1  0.65 1 1 0 cell_1_1 utils::head(point_tbl) #>   point_id x y z value #> 1  point_1 1 1 0  0.65 names(grid) #> [1] "x" "y" "z"

title: Getting Started with XGeoRTR
code: json_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_xgeo_state(state, json_file) restored <- read_xgeo_state(json_file)  restored$attributes$embeddings$active #> [1] "pca_points"

title: Getting Started with XGeoRTR
text: XGeoRTR exposes backend state, derived tables, serialized state exchange. expose scene/camera/viewport APIs renderer-specific orchestration.

title: Popular R Workflows as XGeoRTR Backend States
text: first example turns coefficient-scaled centered predictors explanation values. car becomes point, predictor becomes feature contribution, fitted/residual information becomes point metadata.
code: mt <- datasets::mtcars mt$car <- rownames(mt)  fit_lm <- stats::lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = mt) terms_lm <- c("wt", "hp", "qsec") centered_lm <- scale(mt[, terms_lm, drop = FALSE], center = TRUE, scale = FALSE) contrib_lm <- sweep(centered_lm, 2, stats::coef(fit_lm)[terms_lm], `*`) fitted_lm <- stats::predict(fit_lm) resid_lm <- stats::residuals(fit_lm)  lm_tbl <- data.frame( point_id = rep(mt$car, each = length(terms_lm)), feature = rep(terms_lm, times = nrow(mt)), value = as.vector(t(contrib_lm)), x = rep(scale01(mt$wt), each = length(terms_lm)), y = rep(scale01(fitted_lm), each = length(terms_lm)), z = rep(scale01(abs(resid_lm)), each = length(terms_lm)), response = rep(mt$mpg, each = length(terms_lm)), fitted = rep(fitted_lm, each = length(terms_lm)), residual = rep(resid_lm, each = length(terms_lm)) )  state_lm <- as_xgeo_state( lm_tbl, point_id_col = "point_id", feature_col = "feature", method = "linear-model-coefficient-contributions", meta = list(dataset = "datasets::mtcars", model = "stats::lm") )  state_lm <- finish_state(state_lm) summary(state_lm) #> <summary.xgeo_state> #>   structure:      spatial #>   method:         linear-model-coefficient-contributions #>   points:         32 #>   features:       3 #>   explanations:   96 #>   embeddings:     2 (active: pca_explanations) #>   diagnostics:    1 (active: diagnostics_pca_explanations_explanations) #>   lod bundles:    1 (active: density_grid_pca_explanations) #>   selected points:0 #>   selected feats: 0

title: Popular R Workflows as XGeoRTR Backend States
text: second example uses logistic model stores predicted probability state metadata. gives backend state contract classification workflow.
code: mt$efficient <- as.integer(mt$mpg > stats::median(mt$mpg)) fit_glm <- stats::glm(efficient ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = mt, family = stats::binomial()) #> Warning: glm.fit: algorithm did not converge #> Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred prob_glm <- stats::predict(fit_glm, type = "response") terms_glm <- c("wt", "hp", "qsec") centered_glm <- scale(mt[, terms_glm, drop = FALSE], center = TRUE, scale = FALSE) contrib_glm <- sweep(centered_glm, 2, stats::coef(fit_glm)[terms_glm], `*`)  glm_tbl <- data.frame( point_id = rep(mt$car, each = length(terms_glm)), feature = rep(terms_glm, times = nrow(mt)), value = as.vector(t(contrib_glm)), x = rep(scale01(mt$wt), each = length(terms_glm)), y = rep(prob_glm, each = length(terms_glm)), z = rep(scale01(abs(stats::predict(fit_glm, type = "link"))), each = length(terms_glm)), class = rep(ifelse(mt$efficient == 1L, "high_mpg", "low_mpg"), each = length(terms_glm)), probability = rep(prob_glm, each = length(terms_glm)) )  state_glm <- as_xgeo_state( glm_tbl, point_id_col = "point_id", feature_col = "feature", method = "logistic-model-coefficient-contributions", meta = list(dataset = "datasets::mtcars", model = "stats::glm") )  state_glm <- finish_state(state_glm) summary(state_glm) #> <summary.xgeo_state> #>   structure:      spatial #>   method:         logistic-model-coefficient-contributions #>   points:         32 #>   features:       3 #>   explanations:   96 #>   embeddings:     2 (active: pca_explanations) #>   diagnostics:    1 (active: diagnostics_pca_explanations_explanations) #>   lod bundles:    1 (active: density_grid_pca_explanations) #>   selected points:0 #>   selected feats: 0

title: Popular R Workflows as XGeoRTR Backend States
text: clustering, explanation values can residuals assigned cluster center. geometry can defined PCA layout, explanation table stores feature-level deviations.
code: iris_x <- scale(datasets::iris[, 1:4]) km <- stats::kmeans(iris_x, centers = 3L, nstart = 5L) pca_iris <- stats::prcomp(iris_x, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE) cluster_residual <- iris_x - km$centers[km$cluster, , drop = FALSE]  iris_tbl <- data.frame( point_id = rep(paste0("iris_", seq_len(nrow(iris_x))), each = ncol(iris_x)), feature = rep(colnames(iris_x), times = nrow(iris_x)), value = as.vector(t(cluster_residual)), x = rep(pca_iris$x[, 1], each = ncol(iris_x)), y = rep(pca_iris$x[, 2], each = ncol(iris_x)), z = rep(scale01(km$cluster), each = ncol(iris_x)), species = rep(as.character(datasets::iris$Species), each = ncol(iris_x)), cluster = rep(paste0("cluster_", km$cluster), each = ncol(iris_x)) )  state_km <- as_xgeo_state( iris_tbl, point_id_col = "point_id", feature_col = "feature", method = "kmeans-residual-geometry", meta = list(dataset = "datasets::iris", model = "stats::kmeans") )  state_km <- finish_state(state_km, k = 5L) summary(state_km) #> <summary.xgeo_state> #>   structure:      spatial #>   method:         kmeans-residual-geometry #>   points:         150 #>   features:       4 #>   explanations:   600 #>   embeddings:     2 (active: pca_explanations) #>   diagnostics:    1 (active: diagnostics_pca_explanations_explanations) #>   lod bundles:    1 (active: density_grid_pca_explanations) #>   selected points:0 #>   selected feats: 0

title: Popular R Workflows as XGeoRTR Backend States
text: PCA loadings can also exposed feature-level contributions.
code: arrests <- datasets::USArrests pca_arrests <- stats::prcomp(arrests, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE) scaled_arrests <- scale(arrests, center = pca_arrests$center, scale = pca_arrests$scale) pc1_contrib <- sweep(scaled_arrests, 2, pca_arrests$rotation[, 1], `*`)  pca_tbl <- data.frame( point_id = rep(rownames(arrests), each = ncol(arrests)), feature = rep(colnames(arrests), times = nrow(arrests)), value = as.vector(t(pc1_contrib)), x = rep(pca_arrests$x[, 1], each = ncol(arrests)), y = rep(pca_arrests$x[, 2], each = ncol(arrests)), z = rep(scale01(rowSums(abs(pc1_contrib))), each = ncol(arrests)) )  state_pca <- as_xgeo_state( pca_tbl, point_id_col = "point_id", feature_col = "feature", method = "pca-loading-contribution-geometry", meta = list(dataset = "datasets::USArrests", model = "stats::prcomp") )  state_pca <- finish_state(state_pca, k = 4L) summary(state_pca) #> <summary.xgeo_state> #>   structure:      spatial #>   method:         pca-loading-contribution-geometry #>   points:         50 #>   features:       4 #>   explanations:   200 #>   embeddings:     2 (active: pca_explanations) #>   diagnostics:    1 (active: diagnostics_pca_explanations_explanations) #>   lod bundles:    1 (active: density_grid_pca_explanations) #>   selected points:0 #>   selected feats: 0

title: Popular R Workflows as XGeoRTR Backend States
text: Matrix data can converted state exposed regular grid downstream scientific-visualization packages.
code: state_volcano <- as_xgeo_state(   datasets::volcano,   method = "matrix-regular-grid",   meta = list(dataset = "datasets::volcano") ) state_volcano <- finish_state(state_volcano, embedding_source = "points", k = 4L)  point_tbl <- xgeo_point_values(state_volcano) grid <- xgeo_regular_grid(point_tbl) names(grid) #> [1] "x" "y" "z"

title: Popular R Workflows as XGeoRTR Backend States
text: Serialization writes explicit temporary file.
code: json_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") write_xgeo_state(state_lm, json_file) restored <- read_xgeo_state(json_file)  class(restored) #> [1] "xgeo_state" restored$attributes$embeddings$active #> [1] "pca_explanations"

title: Backend-Neutral Explainable Geometry State and Operators
text: XGeoRTR explainable geometry backend infrastructure R workflows need reusable analytic state rather package-specific presentation code. owns geometry-aware state, embeddings, diagnostics, multiscale summaries, selection state, selected backend tables, JSON state exchange. Downstream packages shapViz3D, rTDA3D, renderer frontends can consume xgeo_state exported backend tables without XGeoRTR taking ownership display, interaction, use-case presentation layers.

title: Backend-Neutral Explainable Geometry State and Operators
text: XGeoRTR geometry/state backend GeoXGL ecosystem, modular R/WebGL stack scalable statistical graphics, explanation geometry, scientific visualization. Within GeoXGL: XGeoRTR owns backend-neutral explanation geometry, xgeo_state, embeddings, diagnostics, level--detail summaries, selection state, public backend tables, JSON state exchange. ggWebGL, available CRAN renderer frontend, owns browser-native WebGL rendering, widgets, viewport interaction, shader execution, static export surfaces. shapViz3D use-case packages domain semantics downstream layout mappings. package rendering, viewport orchestration, camera control, canvas management, shader execution, widget construction, visual themes, export surfaces. responsibilities remain renderer frontends ggWebGL. Compatibility claims read GeoXGL interface contract rather renderer internals.

title: Backend-Neutral Explainable Geometry State and Operators
text: Current scope: backend state, coercion, embeddings, diagnostics, level--detail summaries, selections, explanation tables, point-value tables, regular-grid tables, JSON serialization. Future/prototype scope: topology-specific reasoning, prediction-regime path semantics, large-scale performance claims presented supported concrete downstream examples benchmark artifacts. Causal explanation outside current package scope.

code: library(XGeoRTR)  demo_path <- system.file("extdata", "spatial_demo.csv", package = "XGeoRTR") demo_tbl <- utils::read.csv(demo_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  state <- as_xgeo_state(   demo_tbl,   x_col = "x",   y_col = "y",   z_col = "z",   value_col = "value",   feature_col = "feature",   method = "spatial-field-demo",   meta = list(source = "synthetic-demo", sample_id = "grid-01") )  state <- compute_xgeo_embedding(state, method = "pca", source = "points", dims = 2) state <- set_active_embedding(state, "pca_points") state <- compute_xgeo_diagnostics(state, embedding = "pca_points", source = "points", k = 3) state <- build_xgeo_lod(state, embedding = "pca_points", levels = c(8L, 16L), auto_threshold = 10L) state <- set_xgeo_selection(state, point_ids = state$indices$point_ids[[1]])  summary(state)

title: Backend-Neutral Explainable Geometry State and Operators
text: XGeoRTR ship use-case-specific presentation assets. Shapley-specific semantics belong Shapley-oriented downstream packages, topology-specific semantics belong topology-oriented downstream packages, display orchestration belongs renderer frontends. XGeoRTR provides backend path downstream consumers can use: as_xgeo_state() standardizes long tables set_xgeo_selection() controls subset state upstream xgeo_explanation_table(), xgeo_point_values(), xgeo_regular_grid() expose renderer-neutral tables downstream compute_xgeo_embedding(), compute_xgeo_diagnostics(), build_xgeo_lod() provide optional backend context without taking presentation renderer frontends ggWebGL can convert xgeo_state objects exported backend tables display-specific WebGL specifications without moving rendering responsibilities XGeoRTR backend-example shows intended consumption pattern downstream package. downstream data source unavailable, example falls back bundled spatial_demo.csv backend workflow still runs without SHAP renderer dependency.
code: source("inst/examples/downstream_shapviz3d_state_tables.R")

title: Backend-Neutral Explainable Geometry State and Operators
text: XGeoRTR produces backend state computation products; downstream packages decide present interact products. XGeoRTR: backend geometry/state semantics, operators, selected tables, serialization Downstream consumers: Shapley presentation, TDA workflows, renderer adapters, front-end interaction frozen package boundary documented INTERFACE_FREEZE.md. Public XGeoRTR objects serialized payloads must remain free display-system fields scene, layer, viewport, camera, canvas, theme, shader, widget, export surface metadata.

what: XGeoRTR: Explainable Geometry Backend Infrastructure — XGeoRTR-package
title: XGeoRTR: Explainable Geometry Backend Infrastructure — XGeoRTR-package
text: Backend-level tools standardizing explanation geometries backend-neutral xgeo_state objects embeddings, diagnostics, selection state, multiscale level--detail summaries, selected backend tables, JSON state exchange. XGeoRTR intended serve downstream consumers shapViz3D, rTDA3D, renderer frontends without owning presentation, interaction, adapter layers. frozen package boundary documented INTERFACE_FREEZE.md.

title: XGeoRTR: Explainable Geometry Backend Infrastructure — XGeoRTR-package
text: Maintainer: Frederic Bertrand frederic.bertrand@lecnam.net (ORCID)

title: Build density-grid LOD summaries for a state — build_xgeo_lod
text: state xgeo_state object. embedding Optional embedding name. Defaults active embedding. levels Integer vector grid resolutions. color_by Statistic stored density grid. name Optional LOD bundle name. auto_threshold Point count threshold used lod_level = "auto".

title: Build density-grid LOD summaries for a state — build_xgeo_lod

title: Build density-grid LOD summaries for a state — build_xgeo_lod
code: state <- xgeo_state(matrix(c(1, -1, 2, 0), nrow = 2)) state <- build_xgeo_lod(state, levels = c(4L, 8L), auto_threshold = 2L)  names(state$lod$items) #> [1] "density_grid_spatial"

title: Compute embedding diagnostics for a state — compute_xgeo_diagnostics
code: state <- as_xgeo_state(   data.frame(     point_id = rep(paste0("p", 1:4), each = 2),     feature = rep(c("f1", "f2"), times = 4),     x = c(0, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1),     y = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1),     value = c(0.2, 0.7, 0.4, 0.1, 0.8, 0.6, 0.5, 0.3)   ),   point_id_col = "point_id",   feature_col = "feature" ) state <- compute_xgeo_embedding(state, method = "pca", source = "explanations", dims = 2) state <- compute_xgeo_diagnostics(   state,   embedding = "pca_explanations",   source = "explanations",   k = 1 )  names(state$attributes$diagnostics$items) #> [1] "diagnostics_pca_explanations_explanations"

title: Compute and attach a platform embedding — compute_xgeo_embedding
text: state xgeo_state object. source Source matrix used build embedding. One "explanations", "point_meta", "points". method Embedding backend. "pca" always available; "umap" requires uwot. dims Number output dimensions. name Optional embedding name. Defaults <method>_<source>. ... Passed backend-specific implementations.

title: Compute and attach a platform embedding — compute_xgeo_embedding
code: state <- as_xgeo_state(   data.frame(     point_id = rep(paste0("p", 1:4), each = 2),     feature = rep(c("f1", "f2"), times = 4),     x = c(0, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1),     y = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1),     value = c(0.2, 0.7, 0.4, 0.1, 0.8, 0.6, 0.5, 0.3)   ),   point_id_col = "point_id",   feature_col = "feature" ) state <- compute_xgeo_embedding(state, method = "pca", source = "explanations", dims = 2)  names(state$attributes$embeddings$items) #> [1] "spatial"          "pca_explanations"

title: Set the active embedding on a state — set_active_embedding
code: state <- as_xgeo_state(   data.frame(     point_id = rep(paste0("p", 1:4), each = 2),     feature = rep(c("f1", "f2"), times = 4),     x = c(0, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 1),     y = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1),     value = c(0.2, 0.7, 0.4, 0.1, 0.8, 0.6, 0.5, 0.3)   ),   point_id_col = "point_id",   feature_col = "feature" ) state <- compute_xgeo_embedding(state, method = "pca", source = "explanations", dims = 2) state <- set_active_embedding(state, "pca_explanations")  state$attributes$embeddings$active #> [1] "pca_explanations"

title: Build a long explanation table from backend state — xgeo_explanation_table
code: state <- as_xgeo_state(   data.frame(     point_id = c("p1", "p1", "p2"),     feature = c("f1", "f2", "f1"),     x = c(0, 0, 1),     y = c(0, 0, 1),     value = c(1, -0.5, 0.75),     cluster = c("A", "A", "B")   ),   point_id_col = "point_id",   feature_col = "feature" )  xgeo_explanation_table(state) #>   point_id feature value x y z label cluster #> 1       p1      f1  1.00 0 0 0    f1       A #> 2       p1      f2 -0.50 0 0 0    f2       A #> 3       p2      f1  0.75 1 1 0    f1       B

code: state <- as_xgeo_state(   data.frame(     point_id = c("p1", "p1", "p2", "p2"),     feature = c("f1", "f2", "f1", "f2"),     x = c(0, 0, 1, 1),     y = c(0, 0, 1, 1),     value = c(1, -0.25, 0.75, 2),     cluster = c("A", "A", "B", "B")   ),   point_id_col = "point_id",   feature_col = "feature" ) state <- set_xgeo_selection(state, features = "f1")  xgeo_point_values(state) #>   point_id x y z value cluster #> 1       p1 0 0 0  1.00       A #> 2       p2 1 1 0  0.75       B

text: x matrix, data frame, object coercible backend geometry state. embeddings Optional embedding state. diagnostics Optional diagnostic state. lod Optional level--detail state. selection Optional explicit selection state. metadata Optional state metadata.

title: Create an xgeo_state — xgeo_state
text: xgeo_state object.

title: Create an xgeo_state — xgeo_state
code: # Matrix input is converted into a backend regular grid state. xgeo_state(matrix(c(1, -1, 2, 0), nrow = 2)) #> <xgeo_state> #>   structure:    spatial #>   method:       generic #>   points:       4 #>   features:     1 #>   embeddings:   1 (active: spatial) #>   diagnostics:  0 #>   lod bundles:  0  # Long-tabular input preserves point ids and feature ids. xgeo_state(   data.frame(     point_id = c("p1", "p1", "p2"),     feature = c("f1", "f2", "f1"),     x = c(0, 0, 1),     y = c(0, 0, 1),     value = c(1, -0.5, 0.75)   ) ) #> <xgeo_state> #>   structure:    spatial #>   method:       generic #>   points:       2 #>   features:     2 #>   embeddings:   1 (active: spatial) #>   diagnostics:  0 #>   lod bundles:  0

title: XGeoRTR 0.7.0
text: Added CRAN-safe popular R workflow example script covering stats::lm(), stats::glm(), stats::kmeans(), stats::prcomp(), optional rpart::rpart(), matrix--grid conversion datasets::volcano.

title: XGeoRTR 0.6.0
text: Added runnable, CRAN-safe examples exported help topics package checks longer report checking examples ... NONE. Added documentation coverage tests ensure exported .Rd topics retain \examples{} sections prevent regressions future releases. backend contract public API changes release; update limited documentation quality release-check stability.

title: XGeoRTR 0.5.0
text: Added INTERFACE_FREEZE.md recursive contract tests lock XGeoRTR/ggWebGL ownership boundary. Added renderer-agnostic table helpers: xgeo_explanation_table(), xgeo_point_values(), xgeo_regular_grid(). Centralized selection-aware point/feature filtering downstream packages. Clarified public interface freeze around xgeo_state; use-case packages consume backend state generic tables rather internal data structures.

title: XGeoRTR 0.4.0
text: Hard architectural break: xgeo_scene replaced backend-neutral xgeo_state. Added as_xgeo_state(), write_xgeo_state(), read_xgeo_state(). Added backend accessors: xgeo_geometry(), xgeo_attributes(), xgeo_indices(), xgeo_selection(), xgeo_metadata(). Removed renderer-facing API XGeoRTR: render_webgl(), snapshot_webgl(), render_xgeo_layer(), geom_xgeo_*() constructors. Retargeted embedding/diagnostics/LOD/selection operators xgeo_state. xgeo_data now internal-longer part public API.

title: XGeoRTR 0.2.0
text: Redesigned xgeo_data normalized platform object explicit points, explanations, point_meta, feature_meta, predictions, uncertainty. Preserved unmapped point-level metadata as_xgeo_data.data.frame() ingestion instead dropping extra columns. Expanded xgeo_scene() embeddings, diagnostics, LOD state, views, selection state, scene metadata. Added compute_xgeo_embedding(), compute_xgeo_diagnostics(), build_xgeo_lod(), set_active_embedding(), set_xgeo_selection(), set_xgeo_lod(). Added geom_xgeo_points() geom_xgeo_density() alongside existing geom_xgeo_surface(). Added JSON scene IO write_xgeo_scene() read_xgeo_scene(). Removed misleading public animate_camera_orbit() helper renderer execute camera animation paths release.
